steps.title: Identify the temperature range structure
steps.content: The sentence describes a range spanning from one temperature at one time to another temperature at another time, using “from” and “to” to mark the endpoints.

steps.title: Apply from-to punctuation rules
steps.content: In a “from...to” range, the preposition “from” must connect directly to its first object without intervening punctuation. Choice C maintains this proper connection: “from 18.4°C at 11:00 a.m. to 16.2°C at 7:00 p.m.” flows as a unified range. The other choices either place a comma after “from” (breaking the preposition-object link) or use an incorrect colon. That matches choice C — C is correct.
